loaf of bread. But today, the silence is different. It is wide and bright, like the morning light hitting the tiles of the shop floor.

I look at the flour on my knuckles. It is no longer a shroud; it is just flour. It is the dust of the work that connects us, not the dust of a grave I was digging for myself. I feel the steady thrum of the city through the soles of my shoes—the distant rumble of a
